evals.prompt: Refactor OrderService.java to follow our Java 21 coding conventions. The class has mutable state, uses loops instead of streams, throws checked exceptions, uses null returns, and builds a report via string concatenation. Use records, streams, Optional, switch expressions, text blocks, Stream.toList(), final var, and List.of/copyOf where appropriate. Do not use any Java 22+ features.
evals.expected_output: Refactored OrderService with final fields, constructor injection, streams, Optional, small methods, final var locals, text block report template, Stream.toList()/List.copyOf, no post-Java 21 features.

assertions.text: All local variables and parameters use final (final var allowed)
No checked exceptions in method signatures
Methods that may not find a result return Optional

evals.prompt: Refactor UserProfile.java to follow our Java 21 conventions. It's a mutable JavaBean with getters/setters and imperative collection code. Convert it to a record; defensively copy the roles list in the compact constructor; replace hasRole/getActiveRoles with stream-based implementations.
evals.expected_output: UserProfile is a record with List.copyOf in the compact constructor; stream-based helpers; no setters; no post-Java 21 features.

evals.prompt: Write a Java 21 payment processing module in package com.example.payment. Define a sealed interface PaymentResult permitting three record implementations: Success(String transactionId), Declined(String reason), and Error(String message, Throwable cause). Then write a PaymentDispatcher class with a method 'describe(PaymentResult result)' that returns a String. Use pattern matching for switch (the Java 21 feature) — no default branch; rely on exhaustiveness. Use record patterns to destructure the records. Use final pattern variables and final var locals.
evals.expected_output: sealed interface PaymentResult with three record subtypes; PaymentDispatcher.describe using a switch expression with record patterns, no default, exhaustive. No post-Java 21 features.

assertions.text: Uses switch expressions with arrow syntax
All local variables and parameters use final
